title: Three.js physics domain and interaction ABI vocabulary
description: Executable structural schema for the shared physics ABI. Cross-record physics, graph, conservation, closure, and performance semantics remain assigned to the named semantic validators.

x-abi-relationships.description: PhysicsContext.worldTransformRevision equals WorldPhysicsTransform.transformRevision
PhysicsContext frame, chart, clock, identity, and gravity references resolve in the exact registered revisions and acceleration channel specialization
PhysicsInstant rationalSubstep is reduced and satisfies 0 <= numerator < denominator
PhysicsTime has exactly one active arm
PhysicsClockDescriptor.mapping has exactly the arm named by mappingKind; all other arms are typed-absence
Clock mapping tables use exactly one inline or content-addressed immutable-resource arm and reject out-of-range evaluation
PhysicsInstant.timeSecondsDerived equals the normative evaluation under clockMappingRevision
PhysicsSignalDescriptor channels and perChannelError have identical key sets
Every graph read is justified by one edge and every edge terminates at one matching read
Only committed-publication versions escape a loop or transaction
Interaction payload tag is compatible with its footprint measure
Reaction groups are accepted all-or-none and close in one balance frame/reference point
Every committed stage write belongs to exactly one matching successful PhysicsCommitGroup result
External and GPU-authoritative recovery restores state, inventories, stable IDs, RNG/event/application cursors, then replays exactly once before atomic publication
Candidate contains no camera or render transform
CameraViewPublication references one candidate
ViewPreparationPublication references the same candidate and camera publication
Each camera target/view has exactly one preparation and either one sealed snapshot or one typed failed target execution
ViewPreparationPublication owns full leases only for resources first created by that target/view and may reference Candidate plus same-preparation leases
Snapshot references candidate binding IDs and lease refs instead of copying pairs or transforms
LeaseDisposition.consumingSnapshotIds equals every sealed snapshot that references that lease
FrameExecutionRecord lease dispositions are keyed by leaseId
Physical routes require PhysicsCostLedger and zero steady frame-critical readback
